title: Design System: NEUST Extension Project Monitoring System

title.purpose: Card, dialog, and strong row titles.

body.purpose: Default product copy, fields, and table content.

label.purpose: Metadata, status, support, and navigation detail.

shadows.name: structural-lift
shadows.purpose: Minimal separation for bounded surfaces in the light theme.

shadows.name: night-structural-lift
shadows.purpose: Minimal separation for bounded surfaces in the dark theme.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Color, border, and background feedback for interactive states.

motion.name: state-reveal
motion.purpose: Rare non-blocking reveal where an existing surface requires it.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Near-instant fallback for users who prefer reduced motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The decisive action within a local workflow area.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { height: 36px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 8px; background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-foreground); padding: 0 10px; font: 500 14px/1 'Roboto Variable', sans-serif; transition: background-color 180ms ease, border-color 180ms ease, transform 180ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--brand-primary-hover); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: A secondary action that stays subordinate to the primary path.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { height: 36px; border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); padding: 0 10px; font: 500 14px/1 'Roboto Variable', sans-serif; transition: background-color 180ms ease, border-color 180ms ease; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: var(--muted); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Field
components.description: Compact standard field for search, forms, and filters.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" value="Community Health Program" aria-label="Project title">
components.css: .ds-input { width: 280px; height: 36px; border: 1px solid var(--input); border-radius: 8px; background: transparent; color: var(--foreground); padding: 4px 10px; font: 400 14px/1.5 'Roboto Variable', sans-serif; transition: border-color 180ms ease, box-shadow 180ms ease; } .ds-input:hover { border-color: var(--sea-ink-soft); } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: var(--ring); outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); }

components.name: Work Surface
components.description: A bounded surface for one coherent work object or control area.
components.html:
Quarterly progress report

Review the submission and record the next action.
components.css: .ds-card { width: 320px; border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 12px; background: var(--card); color: var(--card-foreground); padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px var(--shadow-card); font-family: 'Roboto Variable', sans-serif; } .ds-card h3 { margin: 0; font-size: 16px; line-height: 1.5; font-weight: 500; } .ds-card p { margin: 8px 0 0; color: var(--muted-foreground); font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Approved Status
components.description: Plain-language workflow state reinforced by icon and semantic color.
components.html: Approved
components.css: .ds-status { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; height: 22px; border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--background); color: var(--muted-foreground); padding: 2px 6px; font: 500 12px/1 'Roboto Variable', sans-serif; } .ds-status svg { width: 12px; height: 12px; color: var(--success); }

components.name: Active Navigation Item
components.description: Role-specific destination with familiar icon, label, and selected state.
components.html: Dashboard
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; height: 32px; border-radius: 8px; background: var(--sidebar-accent); color: var(--sidebar-accent-foreground); padding: 0 8px; text-decoration: none; font: 400 14px/1 'Roboto Variable', sans-serif; transition: background-color 180ms ease; } .ds-nav-item svg { width: 16px; height: 16px; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: var(--muted); } .ds-nav-item:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--sidebar-ring) 50%, transparent); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Metric Summary
components.description: Compact operational context, never a decorative hero metric.
components.css: .ds-metric { display: flex; min-height: 104px; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 12px; background: var(--card); color: var(--card-foreground); padding: 16px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px var(--shadow-card); font-family: 'Roboto Variable', sans-serif; } .ds-metric span { color: var(--muted-foreground); font-size: 14px; line-height: 16px; } .ds-metric strong { margin-top: auto; color: var(--heading); font-size: 30px; line-height: 1; font-weight: 600; }

narrative.overview: EPMS should feel like work moving visibly and steadily through one connected institutional flow. Its visual language is calm, dependable, and direct: familiar controls, compact hierarchy, and explicit states keep faculty focused on the next required action while giving reviewing roles trustworthy oversight. The system uses restrained color, gently curved geometry, and role-aware navigation rather than spectacle. It explicitly rejects the generic SaaS dashboard: decorative metrics, excessive card grids, gradients, and startup-style flourishes must never compete with proposal, monitoring, or reporting work.
keyCharacteristics: Restrained sea-ink and lagoon palette with semantic status colors.
Compact Roboto hierarchy optimized for forms, tables, and workflow labels.
Structural borders and tonal layers with minimal shadow.
Familiar, decisive controls with visible hover, focus, disabled, loading, and error states.
Responsive app shell with role-specific navigation and content-first mobile behavior.

rules.name: The Current Rule
rules.body: Every screen must reveal where the work is, who owns it, and what happens next.

rules.name: The Accent Earns Its Place Rule
rules.body: Lagoon is reserved for primary action, focus, selection, links, and state. Never use it as ambient decoration.

rules.name: The Semantic Color Rule
rules.body: Success, warning, destructive, and informational colors must always retain their established meaning and must be reinforced by text or iconography.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Never introduce a display face into product labels, buttons, tables, or forms. Roboto carries the system.

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale Rule
rules.body: Product typography uses fixed rem sizes. Do not use fluid display scaling inside the authenticated application shell.

rules.name: The Border-First Rule
rules.body: If a surface can be understood through background and a one-pixel border, no additional shadow is allowed.

dos: Do make current status, owner, and next action visible without opening a secondary panel.
Do use Lagoon Deep (#328f97) only for primary action, focus, selection, links, and state.
Do preserve the same control shape and interaction vocabulary across faculty, RET Chair, Director, and administrator screens.
Do use skeletons for content loading and instructive empty states that explain the next available action.
Do maintain WCAG 2.2 AA contrast, keyboard behavior, visible focus, robust labels, and reduced-motion alternatives.
donts: Don't resemble a generic SaaS dashboard: decorative metrics, excessive card grids, gradients, and startup-style visual flourishes are prohibited.
Don't nest cards or repeat identical icon-heading-copy cards as page scaffolding.
Don't use gradient text, glassmorphism, thick colored side stripes, or arbitrary z-index values.
Don't use lagoon, success, warning, or destructive colors as decoration detached from action or state.
Don't invent custom affordances when a standard button, field, table, menu, tooltip, or dialog already communicates the task.
Don't animate product content for spectacle; motion exists only to explain state and must stop or simplify under reduced-motion preferences.
